nodes.title: Step Function Approximation
nodes.label: thm:step-function-approximation
nodes.text_preview: [Step Function Approximation] Let $f:[a,b] $ be continuous and let $ >0$. Then there exists a step function $s_ :[a,b] $ such that \[ _ x |f(x)-s_ (x)|< . \]

nodes.title: Piecewise Linear Approximation
nodes.label: thm:piecewise-linear-approximation
nodes.text_preview: [Piecewise Linear Approximation] Let $f $ be continuous and let $ >0$. Then there exists a continuous piecewise linear function $ _ $ such that $ _ x |f(x)- _ (x)|< $.

nodes.title: Weierstrass Approximation Theorem
nodes.label: thm:weierstrass-approximation
nodes.text_preview: [Weierstrass Approximation Theorem] Let $f:[a,b] $ be continuous. For every $ >0$ there exists a polynomial $p_ [x]$ such that $\|f-p_ \|_ ,[a,b] < $.

nodes.title: Bernstein Polynomial
nodes.label: def:bernstein-polynomial
nodes.text_preview: [Bernstein Polynomial] Let $n $ and let $f:[0,1] $. A function $B_n:[0,1] $ is called the $n$th Bernstein polynomial of $f$ if and only if, for every $x [0,1]$, \[ B_n(x)= _ k=0 ^ n f\! ( n ) k x^k(1-x)^ n-k . \]

nodes.title: Bernstein Approximation Theorem
nodes.label: thm:bernstein-approximation
nodes.text_preview: [Bernstein Approximation Theorem] Let $f:[0,1] $ be continuous, and for each $n $ let $B_n$ denote the $n$th Bernstein polynomial of $f$. Then for every $ >0$ there exists $n_ $ such that $\|f-B_n\|_ < $ for all $n n_ $.

nodes.label: def:tagged-partition

nodes.title: Gauge
nodes.label: def:gauge
nodes.text_preview: [Gauge] Let $a,b $ with $a<b$ and set $I=[a,b]$. A gauge on $I$ is a function $ :I (0, )$.

nodes.title: Cousin's Theorem
nodes.label: thm:cousins-theorem
nodes.text_preview: [Cousin's Theorem] Every gauge $ :[a,b] (0, )$ on a closed interval $[a,b]$ admits a $ $-fine tagged partition of $[a,b]$.

nodes.title: Mesh of a Partition
nodes.label: def:mesh-of-partition
nodes.text_preview: [Mesh of a Partition] Let $a,b $ with $a<b$, and let $P=\ x_0, ,x_n\ $ be a partition of $[a,b]$. A real number $m$ is the (or ) of $P$ if and only if \[ m= _ 1 i n (x_i - x_ i-1 ). \] The mesh of $P$ is denoted by $\|P\

nodes.title: Refinement
nodes.label: def:refinement-of-partition
nodes.text_preview: [Refinement] Let [a,b] and let P and Q be partitions of [a,b]. Then Q is a refinement of P if and only if every point of P is also a point of Q; equivalently, P Q.

nodes.title: Common Refinement
nodes.label: def:common-refinement
nodes.text_preview: [Common Refinement] Let a<b and let P and Q be partitions of [a,b]. Their common refinement is the partition R obtained by taking the union of their node sets, so R = P Q as sets of points in [a,b].

nodes.title: Bounded on a Set
nodes.label: def:bounded-on-a-set
nodes.text_preview: [Bounded on a Set] Let $A $ and $f A $. The function $f$ is bounded on $A$ if and only if there exists a constant $M>0$ such that $|f(x)| M$ for every $x A$.

nodes.title: Boundedness Theorem
nodes.label: thm:boundedness-theorem
nodes.text_preview: [Boundedness Theorem] Let $a<b$ and let $f:[a,b] $ be continuous on $[a,b]$. Then there exists $M>0$ such that $|f(x)| M$ for every $x $.

nodes.title: Absolute Maximum and Absolute Minimum
nodes.label: def:absolute-extrema

nodes.title: Extreme Value Theorem
nodes.label: thm:extreme-value-theorem
nodes.text_preview: [Extreme Value Theorem] Let $a,b $ with $a<b$, and let $f:[a,b] $ be continuous. There exist points $x_m,x_M [a,b]$ such that $f(x_m) f(x) f(x_M)$ for every $x [a,b]$. Equivalently, $f$ attains both an absolute maximum a

nodes.title: Location of Roots
nodes.label: thm:location-of-roots
nodes.text_preview: [Location of Roots] Let $a,b $ with $a<b$ and let $f:[a,b] $ be continuous. If either $f(a)<0<f(b)$ or $f(a)>0>f(b)$, then there exists $c (a,b)$ such that $f(c)=0$.

nodes.title: Bolzano's Intermediate Value Theorem
nodes.label: thm:bolzano-intermediate-value
nodes.text_preview: [Bolzano's Intermediate Value Theorem] Let $I $ be an interval, let $f I $ be continuous on $I$, and choose $a,b I$ with $f(a) < k < f(b)$. Then there exists $c I$ such that $ \ a,b\ < c < \ a,b\ $ and $f(c) = k$.

nodes.title: Preservation of Intervals
nodes.label: thm:preservation-of-intervals
nodes.text_preview: [Preservation of Intervals] Let $I $ be an interval and let $f:I $ be continuous. Then $f(I)$ is an interval.

nodes.title: Darboux Property
nodes.label: thm:darboux-property
nodes.text_preview: [Darboux Property] Let a,b satisfy a<b, let f:[a,b] be continuous, and fix c . If f(x) c for every x , then either f(x)>c for all x or f(x)<c for all x .

nodes.label: thm:monotone-one-sided-limits
nodes.text_preview: [One-Sided Limits of Monotone Functions] Let $I $ be an interval, let $c$ be interior to $I$, and let $f:I $ be increasing. Define \[ L_- \ f(x): x I,\; x<c\ , L_+ \ f(x): x I,\; c<x\ . \] Then both one-sided limits exis

nodes.title: Jump of a Function
nodes.label: def:jump-of-function
nodes.text_preview: [Jump of a Function] Let $I $ be an interval, let $f:I $ be increasing, and let $c$ be an interior point of $I$. If the one-sided limits $f(c^-)$ and $f(c^+)$ exist, the jump of $f$ at $c$ is \[ j_f(c) f(c^+)-f(c^-). \]

nodes.title: Jump Intervals Are Disjoint
nodes.label: cor:jump-intervals-for-monotone-discontinuities

proof_file_blocks.title: Jump Intervals of a Monotone Function Are Disjoint

nodes.text_preview: [Jump Intervals Are Disjoint] Let $I $ be an interval and let $f:I $ be nondecreasing. If $a,b I$ are distinct points of discontinuity of $f$, then the open intervals $(f(a^-),f(a^+))$ and $(f(b^-),f(b^+))$ are disjoint.

nodes.label: thm:monotone-discontinuities-countable

proof_file_blocks.title: Discontinuities of a Monotone Function Are Countable

nodes.text_preview: [Discontinuities of a Monotone Function Are Countable] Let $I $ be an interval and let $f:I $ be monotone. Then the set $D_f \ c I : f c\ $ is at most countable.

nodes.label: cor:monotone-continuous-on-dense-set

proof_file_blocks.title: Monotone Function Is Continuous on a Dense Set

nodes.text_preview: [Monotone Function Is Continuous on a Dense Set] Let $f:[a,b] $ be monotone. Then the set $\ x :f x\ $ is dense in $[a,b]$.

nodes.title: Continuous Injective Is Strictly Monotone
nodes.label: prop:continuous-injective-iff-strictly-monotone
nodes.text_preview: [Continuous Injective Is Strictly Monotone] Let $f:[a,b] $ be continuous on $[a,b]$. Then $f$ is injective if and only if $f$ is strictly monotone on $[a,b]$.

nodes.title: Continuous Inverse Theorem
nodes.label: thm:continuous-inverse-theorem
nodes.text_preview: [Continuous Inverse Theorem] Let $I $ be an interval and let $f:I $ be strictly monotone and continuous on $I$. Then the inverse function $f^ -1 :f(I) I$ exists, is strictly monotone, and is continuous on $f(I)$.

nodes.title: Point of Discontinuity
nodes.label: def:point-of-discontinuity
nodes.text_preview: [Point of Discontinuity] Let $A $, let $f A $, and let $a A$. The function $f$ is discontinuous at $a$ if and only if $f$ is not continuous at $a$.

nodes.label: def:sequential-discontinuity-at-a-point

nodes.label: def:oscillation-on-a-set

nodes.title: Uniform Continuity
nodes.label: def:uniform-continuity
nodes.text_preview: [Uniform Continuity] Let $A $ and let $f:A $. The function $f$ is uniformly continuous on $A$ if and only if for every $ > 0$ there exists $ > 0$ such that for all $x,u A$ the implication $|x-u| < |f(x)-f(u)| < $ holds.

nodes.title: Heine--Cantor Theorem
nodes.label: thm:heine-cantor
nodes.text_preview: [Heine--Cantor Theorem] Let $a,b $ with $a<b$ and let $f:[a,b] $ be continuous on $[a,b]$. Then $f$ is uniformly continuous on $[a,b]$.

nodes.title: Lipschitz Condition
nodes.label: def:lipschitz-condition
nodes.text_preview: [Lipschitz Condition] Let $A $, let $f:A $, and let $K>0$. The function $f$ satisfies the Lipschitz condition on $A$ with constant $K$ if and only if $|f(x)-f(u)| K|x-u|$ for every $x,u A$.
